reasoning: The Lean signature faithfully captures the informal claim. `IsNilpotent (I : Ideal R)` unfolds to `∃ n, I^n = 0`, which is equivalent to "∃ n ≥ 1, I^n = 0" since `I^0 = ⊤` (equal to 0 only in trivial rings, where n=1 also works). The `span ({↑p} : Set (ZMod (p^m)))` with `open Ideal` is `Ideal.span`, which produces the principal ideal generated by `(p : ZMod (p^m))` — exactly $p\mathbb{Z}/p^m\mathbb{Z}$ as a subset of $\mathbb{Z}/p^m\mathbb{Z}$. The ambient ring `ZMod (p^m)` matches $\mathbb{Z}/p^m\mathbb{Z}$. The `hp : p.Prime` hypothesis surfaces the standard convention that "p" denotes a prime; no hypothesis on m is needed since the statement holds trivially when m = 0.
